verdict_reasoning: CRISP1 is a secreted epididymal protein that binds to the sperm surface — specifically the postacrosomal/equatorial segment involved in sperm-egg fusion. While it lacks its own TM domain or GPI anchor, it associates in a wash-resistant, stable manner with the sperm plasma membrane during epididymal transit, forming a stable surface coat that persists through fertilization. This qualifies as tissue_restricted_surface (sperm/epididymis-specific) with stable_surface_attachment characteristics: the protein is deposited onto sperm during epididymal transit and remains on the outer surface of the sperm plasma membrane through fertilization. The display is real but restricted to the narrow germline/gamete lineage. Not expressed as a canonical TM protein in somatic cells; no documented cancer ecto-pool, cell-death surfacing, lysosomal exocytosis, or activation-induced translocation in somatic cells.
predicted_key_uncertainty: Uncertain whether sperm-surface binding is truly wash-resistant (stable_surface_attachment) vs. reversible adsorption; the extent of stable vs. dynamic association in the sperm coat has some ambiguity in the literature.
